mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust labor market, which drives consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive equity returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment is a core driver of aggregate demand.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in PAYEMS shows a rank correlation coefficient below 0.05 with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates past 3%, it signals eroding purchasing power and prompts expectations of monetary tightening, increasing discount rates and negatively impacting equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of slowing industrial production (INDPRO) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates a stagflationary environment. This simultaneously pressures corporate profit margins and raises discount rates, creating a strongly negative outlook for equities.
candidate_trigger: 6-month change in INDPRO < 0 AND 6-month change in CPIAUCSL > 0
evidence_refs: fact:1970s stagflationary period was poor for equity returns.
failure_condition: A model based on the interaction of INDPRO and CPIAUCSL changes fails to outperform a simple additive model in walk-forward validation over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust household formation and confidence, driving broad economic activity and positively impacting forward equity returns, particularly during economic recoveries.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a key leading indicator of the business cycle.
failure_condition: The signal's positive effect disappears or reverses during periods when FEDFUNDS is above 4%, indicating the mechanism is not robust to monetary tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real retail sales (RSAFS, adjusted for CPIAUCSL) signals strong consumer demand, which is the primary driver of GDP and corporate revenue growth. This leads to positive equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for roughly two-thirds of U.S. GDP.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between 3-month real RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE), defined as a rise of more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, is a reliable leading indicator of a recession, causing a sharp decline in equity prices.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 posts positive 60-day forward returns, invalidating its reliability as a negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, negative month-over-month shock to industrial production (INDPRO) signals an unexpected disruption in the economy's supply side, leading to immediate negative revisions of growth forecasts and short-term negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a high-frequency, coincident indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 4%, the high opportunity cost of holding equities (relative to cash/bonds) and the restrictive stance of monetary policy weigh on valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Higher risk-free rates increase the discount rate for future earnings.
failure_condition: The median 20-day forward return when FEDFUNDS is above 4% is not lower than the median return when it is below 4% over a 20-year period.
